synthesis.summary: Ford surged 15% on extremely heavy volume (6.8x normal), but the only available news is a medium-impact Benzinga piece citing "energy business optimism" — too vague to fully account for a move of this magnitude. A catalyst of this size typically requires a major earnings beat, significant guidance raise, or structural announcement; the supplied news does not confirm any of those mechanisms.

COHR is up 8.6% on the day and nearly 18% over five days with no direct news in the supplied items explaining the move — the Tower Semiconductor earnings story relates to a peer, not Coherent. The move likely reflects sector rotation or sympathy buying into AI/photonics infrastructure names following strong results from semiconductor peers, but no company-specific catalyst is present in the supplied news.

AAOI surged 22% on heavy relative volume with RSI hitting overbought territory, but none of the supplied news items address the company directly. The only news available covers a leveraged ETF launch for Seagate and SanDisk, which has no direct bearing on AAOI. The move is likely driven by options flow, short squeeze dynamics, or sector rotation not captured in the available news.

AUR surged 17.65% on 1.59x relative volume with RSI hitting 86, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The catalyst — whether options activity, sector rotation, short squeeze, or an untracked announcement — is not identifiable from available data.

CCCC is up 12.8% on 6.4x normal volume with no company-specific news in the supplied feed. The only available items cover broad market strength (Nasdaq +1%) and a generic "stocks moving higher" roundup — neither explains a move of this magnitude or the unusual volume spike. This is likely driven by options flow, short covering, or sector rotation not visible in the news feed.

CAI dropped 14.4% today on 2.14x relative volume, extending a 25% five-day decline, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The elevated volume and RSI near oversold territory suggest forced selling, options unwinding, or a catalyst not captured in the available news feed.

EVLV dropped 15.35% on 4x relative volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The elevated volume suggests a motivated seller or options-related flow, but without a catalyst this remains unattributed.

FCEL is up 17% today and 57% over the past five days with RSI at 81, but the only available news is a generic whale-alert roundup for industrials stocks — nothing specific to FuelCell Energy. The move is likely driven by options flow, short-squeeze dynamics, or sector rotation that isn't visible in the supplied news.

MBLY is up 16% on 2.6x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The surge could reflect options activity, short covering (MBLY has had elevated short interest historically), or sector rotation into autonomous/ADAS names, but none of these can be confirmed from available inputs.

PLUG surged 14% on heavy volume following an earnings report that prompted multiple analysts to raise their forecasts, signaling improved expectations for the company's financial trajectory. The 2.52x relative volume and RSI of 74.5 confirm broad market participation, consistent with a post-earnings momentum burst. The 5-day gain of 23% suggests the move began building into or around the earnings event.

REPL surged 30.6% on the day with volume running 2.2x normal, but the only supplied news is a generic healthcare sector roundup with no specific catalyst for the company. A move of this magnitude almost certainly stems from an undisclosed binary event — clinical trial data, an FDA decision, or a significant partnership announcement — none of which appear in the provided news.

RCAT dropped 14.8% on 3.3x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The elevated volume and RSI near oversold territory (35) suggest either forced selling, options-related flows, or broader sector rotation — none of which are visible in the available data.

SLS is up ~25% on volume more than 8x normal, but none of the supplied news items explain the move — the only coverage is a generic "stocks moving higher" roundup with low impact that provides no company-specific catalyst. A move of this magnitude in a small-cap name with RSI above 70 typically signals a binary catalyst (clinical data, regulatory decision, or short squeeze), none of which appear in the available news.

Tower Semiconductor's operating profit nearly doubled, driven by AI-related demand for its analog and specialty chips — a magnitude of earnings improvement that directly explains the ~20% single-day surge. Volume was roughly in line with average, suggesting the move is price discovery on the earnings result rather than a liquidity event, and the RSI pushing into overbought territory is consistent with a sharp post-earnings re-rating.

VSH is up 13.7% on roughly double normal volume, pushing RSI above 81, but the only news supplied is a low-impact Benzinga roundup noting the stock is moving higher — it describes the move without explaining its cause. No earnings, guidance change, M&A, or analyst event is present in the input to account for a move of this magnitude.

WOLF is up ~19% today with nearly 3x normal volume and an RSI of 87.8, but none of the supplied news items contain a company-specific catalyst — the available articles are low-impact roundups that merely note the stock is moving. The actual driver (earnings, contract, guidance change, short squeeze, or institutional flow) is not present in the provided news. The 5-day gain of ~49% suggests a multi-day squeeze or momentum event that also lacks a documented trigger here.

XOM gained 0.49% on below-average volume (rel. volume 0.69) with no identifiable news catalyst. The modest gain and neutral RSI (48.8) suggest routine sector drift or broad market flows rather than a company-specific event.

INTC is essentially flat today (+0.03%) despite a strong 5-day run of +6.72%. No news in the supplied feed explains either the recent surge or today's stall. The overbought RSI (75) and below-average relative volume (0.56) suggest the prior move may have been driven by flows or sector rotation that aren't visible here.

KO is up 0.31% on below-average volume (rel. volume 0.88) with no news catalysts supplied. The modest gain is consistent with routine sector rotation or broad market drift rather than any company-specific event.

NVDA rose 2.44% to a record high, driven by broad tech momentum as the Nasdaq 100 climbed despite hotter-than-expected PPI data, with geopolitical optimism from Trump's China visit adding macro tailwinds. Relative volume is below average (0.62x), suggesting this is largely a market-level lift rather than NVDA-specific buying, and the RSI at 72.8 flags the stock as technically overbought.

RKLB is up 7.3% today and nearly 50% over the past five days, but the only available news is a generic "whale alerts" roundup with no RKLB-specific catalyst. The move — which has pushed RSI to overbought territory — is not explained by the supplied news and likely reflects options flow, momentum chasing, or a catalyst not captured in these items.
